diagrams.title: C++ File I/O Stream Class Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Shows the inheritance hierarchy of C++ stream classes including ifstream, ofstream, and fstream, illustrating their relationship with base classes ios_base, istream, ostream, and iostream.
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand which stream classes to use for input (reading), output (writing), or both operations, and how they inherit functionality from base classes.

diagrams.title: File Open Modes in C++
diagrams.description: A visual matrix showing all available file open modes (ios::in, ios::out, ios::app, ios::trunc, ios::binary, ios::ate) and their effects when opening files for reading or writing.
elements: check marks/x marks indicating valid combinations
diagrams.purpose: Explains which mode to use for different file operations - creating new files, appending data, overwriting, or reading existing files.

diagrams.title: File Read/Write Data Flow
diagrams.description: A flow diagram showing how data moves between the external file on disk, through the file stream buffer, into the C++ program variables, and vice versa for writing operations.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the complete lifecycle of file operations - opening a file, reading/writing data through buffers, and closing the file - helping students understand why changes may not appear immediately.
